creator: bugzilla@soulrebel.in-berlin.de
text:
During my normal KDE4-Session randomly everything just gets really, really slow.

After a couple of seconds when things start responding again and I get a fresh output from top I see that Xorg-CPU-Usage slowly goes down from >90% to ~8% again.

I thought it was KDE's fault first, but its Xorg eating CPU-cycles and I do get a lot of errors in my Xorg.0.log.

It is basically full of these lines (thousands of them):
tossed event which came in late
mieqEnequeue: out-of-order valuator event; dropping.

I always have one block of this:
(II) intel(0): EDID vendor "SEC", prod id 17495
(II) intel(0): Printing DDC gathered Modelines:
(II) intel(0): Modeline "1440x900"x0.0   95.44  1440 1504 1536 1744  900 903 906 912 -hsync -vsync (54.7 kHz)
(II) intel(0): EDID vendor "SEC", prod id 17495
(EE) intel(0): underrun on pipe B!

Yesterday I also got one block of this:
(II) AIGLX: Suspending AIGLX clients for VT switch
(II) intel(0): xf86UnbindGARTMemory: unbind key 1
(II) intel(0): xf86UnbindGARTMemory: unbind key 2
(II) intel(0): xf86UnbindGARTMemory: unbind key 3
(II) intel(0): xf86UnbindGARTMemory: unbind key 4
(II) intel(0): xf86UnbindGARTMemory: unbind key 5
(II) intel(0): [drm] removed 1 reserved context for kernel
(II) intel(0): [drm] unmapping 8192 bytes of SAREA 0xc44f9000 at 0x28663000
(II) intel(0): [drm] Closed DRM master.




I have attached an Xorg.0.log from yesterday that is a little smaller than the current one.



My xorg.conf is pretty standard with those extra lines in the device section:
        Option "Accel" "True" 
        Option "AccelMethod" "EXA" 
        Option "MigrationHeuristic" "greedy" 
(they are supposed to help kde4-performance)
and those two in Serverlayout:
        Option          "AIGLX" "true
        Option "GLXWithComposite" "Enable"


Thank you for your help!

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: So far I have only experienced the problems when 3D-Effects for the Desktop are turned on. However, slowdowns seem not be connected directly to effects. Sometimes the effects work fine (and with good performance) for over an hour, then not having done anything, just switching focus from one window to another slows everything down.
